q: Two jobs are MERGEing the same Delta table simultaneously. One fails with ConcurrentAppendException. What caused it and how do you prevent it?
a: Delta uses optimistic concurrency: each writer reads the current snapshot, stages new Parquet files, then races to commit the next version number to the transaction log. At commit time Delta checks whether any files it read have been added or modified by a concurrent writer. When two MERGE jobs scan overlapping sets of files — even on logically different rows — the second commit sees that the files it read have already changed and throws ConcurrentAppendException. The fix is partition isolation: include the partition column in the MERGE ON clause so each writer's file scan is constrained to disjoint partitions and the commit validator sees no overlap. Where true overlap is unavoidable (both jobs can write the same rows), serialize the writers or implement retry-with-backoff since idempotent MERGEs are safe to replay. On recent runtimes, enabling deletion vectors gives row-level conflict detection, reducing false conflicts between writers touching different rows in the same file. The wrong answer is 'Delta can't handle concurrent writes' — it can, as long as file sets are disjoint.
fj: Delta doesn't support concurrent writes, so I need to serialize everything.
fs: Delta handles concurrent writes via optimistic concurrency — conflicts only occur when writers scan overlapping files. Partition-isolate your MERGEs (include the partition col in ON) so file sets are disjoint and commits never conflict.

code.label: Overlap: both jobs scan all files
lines: -- Job A and Job B both run:
MERGE INTO events t USING src s

code.label: Partition-isolated: disjoint file sets
lines: MERGE INTO events t USING src_us s
